heading: Technical assistance, training, and evaluation
label: 10619 Technical assistance, training, and evaluation
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 3797u–8 of Title 42

Amendments2016—Subsec. (a). inserted before period at end “, including training for drug court personnel and officials on identifying and addressing co-occurring substance abuse and mental health problems”.Pub. L. 114–255
amendments: Amendments2016—Subsec. (a). inserted before period at end “, including training for drug court personnel and officials on identifying and addressing co-occurring substance abuse and mental health problems”.Pub. L. 114–255

children.heading: Technical assistance and training
children.label: a Technical assistance and training
children.text: The Attorney General may provide technical assistance and training in furtherance of the purposes of this subchapter, including training for drug court personnel and officials on identifying and addressing co-occurring substance abuse and mental health problems.

children.heading: Evaluations
children.label: b Evaluations
children.text: In addition to any evaluation requirements that may be prescribed for grantees (including uniform data collection standards and reporting requirements), the Attorney General shall carry out or make arrangements for evaluations of programs that receive support under this subchapter.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: c Administration
children.text: The technical assistance, training, and evaluations authorized by this section may be carried out directly by the Attorney General, in collaboration with the Secretary of Health and Human Services, or through grants, contracts, or other cooperative arrangements with other entities.
